channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: High Costs and Cheap Imports Under Pressure for South Africa's Sugarcane Farmers
item.description: South Africa's sugarcane farmers, particularly in KwaZulu-Natal and Mpumalanga, are facing mounting pressures due to rising farming costs and a flood of subsidised sugar imports. The global increase i...

item.title: Lesotho runner Naheng Khatala targets another podium at 2026 Two Oceans Ultra Marathon
item.description: Lesotho long-distance runner Naheng Khatala is set to compete in the Totalsports Two Oceans Ultra Marathon in Cape Town on Saturday, aiming for back-to-back podium finishes after placing third in her ...

item.title: Four migrants die after small boat sinks in the Channel amid continuing rescue efforts
item.description: Four people have lost their lives after a motorised dinghy carrying migrants capsized in the English Channel on Thursday morning. French local authorities confirmed the fatalities and announced that s...

item.title: Filmmaking student fatally stabbed at Primrose Hill
item.description: A 21-year-old filmmaking student, Finbar Sullivan, was fatally stabbed in Primrose Hill, north London, on Tuesday evening. Sullivan, a student at the London Screen Academy, was attacked in a fight nea...

item.title: Trump Voices Criticism of NATO Following Meeting with Alliance Leader
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump expressed strong criticism of NATO following a meeting with the organization's top official. Trump reiterated his long-standing stance that the alliance is burdened ...

item.title: Cheviot Private Equity Fund Set for $150 Million First Close
item.description: Cheviot, a new private equity firm co-founded by Nick Speer, a former dealmaker at Affinity Equity Partners, and John Russell, ex-CEO of Myer Family Investments, is preparing for its inaugural fund cl...

item.title: US-Iran-Israel Ceasefire: Concerns and Suspicion Persist
item.description: The recent ceasefire between Israel and Iran, brokered with US involvement, isn't being taken at face value by either side. Both Washington and Tehran are seriously questioning whether this temporary ...

item.title: NNPC Say Improved Pipeline Security Raise Nigeria’s Oil Production to 1.84 Million Barrels Per Day
item.description: The Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited don yan say crude oil production for the country don jump reach about 1.84 million barrels per day, as pipeline security for the Niger Delta region don ...

item.title: LAPD Officer-Involved Shooting Ends Pursuit on 5 Freeway, Leaving One Dead
item.description: A police pursuit through downtown Los Angeles ended in tragedy on Tuesday morning when a suspect was shot and killed by officers along the 5 Freeway near Olympic Boulevard in East L.A. According to th...

item.title: Lagos Mainland APC Insists on Open Primaries for 2027 House of Representatives Election
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, key members of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Lagos Mainland have turned down a proposal to grant the incumbent House of Representatives member, Moshood Ol...

item.title: PM Modi accuses Mamata Banerjee's TMC government of corruption and neglect during Bengal rally
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a strong attack on West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and her Trinamool Congress (TMC) government while addressing a public rally in Haldia. He accused th...

item.title: Cape Town Woman Rescued from Drain Following Fall in Bonteheuwel
item.description: A dramatic rescue unfolded in Bonteheuwel, Cape Town, on Wednesday evening when a woman fell into a drain near Bofors Circle. Residents alerted authorities after hearing her cries for help, and a swif...

item.title: Woman rescued after crawling 1km through stormwater drain following fall at construction site
item.description: A woman was rescued on Wednesday night after crawling nearly 1km through a stormwater drain in Epping, Cape Town. The incident occurred at a construction site, believed to be cordoned off by PRASA, wh...

item.title: Fatal shooting in Newfoundland leaves one dead and another seriously injured
item.description: Authorities in Newfoundland and Labrador are investigating a shooting incident that resulted in one fatality and left another individual seriously injured. The circumstances surrounding the incident a...

item.title: Urban Wildlife Shows Convergent Behaviors Across Cities
item.description: Urban wildlife across the globe is exhibiting remarkably similar behaviors due to the influence of city environments. Animals such as monkeys in New Delhi, squirrels in New York, and white ibises in S...

item.title: Mudavadi Calls for Practical Urban Planning Solutions at AUF2 in Nairobi
item.description: Kenya is hosting the Second Africa Urban Forum (AUF2) at the Kenyatta International Convention Centre, bringing together leaders, policymakers, city officials, and development experts from across Afri...

item.title: Four Migrants Die in Channel Crossing as France Rejects UK Proposal for Boat Interceptions
item.description: At least four migrants have tragically lost their lives while attempting to cross the English Channel early this morning. The boat, which was overcrowded with dozens of people, ran into trouble off th...

item.title: Abbotsford Rest Stop Encampment Cleared by B.C. Government After Years of Concerns
item.description: The British Columbia government has recently cleared a homeless encampment situated at a Whatcom Road Park and Ride rest stop in Abbotsford. The encampment, which had been in place for approximately t...

item.title: Kenyan Law Cracks Down on ID Retention by Lenders and Employers
item.description: The Kenyan government is clamping down on the common practice of lenders, employers, and landlords holding onto national identity cards (IDs) as collateral or security. Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipc...

item.title: Ben Harvey tests mouthwash impact on alcohol readings
item.description: In the latest edition of 'Up Late', host Ben Harvey conducts a hands-on experiment with Listerine mouthwash to investigate its effect on breath alcohol tests. This experiment follows an incident where...
